text: My dad's brain tumor is officially in remission. We're so incredibly relieved, but I can't shake this feeling of dread. What if it comes back? Every little headache, every moment of fatigue sends my mind racing. I know we should be celebrating, but all I can think about are the possibilities for the future and how fragile everything feels right now.
It's back. They said it was gone but it came roaring back like a monster. I don't know what to do anymore. I can barely breathe.

The oncologist confirmed what I'd feared - the cancer is back. It feels like we just finished the last round of treatment, and now we have to face this all over again.  I try to be strong for Sarah, but there are moments when I feel completely overwhelmed. How many times can a person fight this battle?

My oncologist mentioned that my tumor is becoming more difficult to manage, and it's hard not to wonder what that means for the future. I'm still feeling fairly well overall, so I'm trying to stay focused on each day as it comes.

This whole palliative care thing is scary. U really start thinking about what happens next. I keep picturing him lying there, alone.  His favorite baseball team lost last night - they're doing terrible rn. How can anything else matter right now?
My chest feels like it's going to explode every time I think about what's happening.

Called Mom today, told her I'm okay but that's getting harder and harder. My head is foggy so much of the time now, it feels like I'm wading through mud. What if this gets worse? What if they can't do anything else for me? I just keep thinking about all the things I wanted to do, see... will I even get a chance?
Still can't believe John's gone rn. Miss his laugh something fierce.  Been forgetting to feed the cat lately tho, gotta get it together.

It's been weeks since Tom passed and I still can't shake this feeling that it wasn't supposed to end like this. The lunches we used to share at work feel so empty now. He fought so hard.  I just wish things could have gone differently.
It's been really rough since [Name]'s diagnosis. I keep thinking about all the things we talked about doing, and it just hurts.
I just spoke to the doctor again. It's so hard knowing there aren't many options left for my child. My heart aches constantly. I have to stay strong though, for them.

My little one is sleeping now, so peaceful...it just makes everything worse rn.  How'd we get here? I keep thinking about all the fun things we were supposed to do this summer. Stupid leaky faucet in the bathroom won't stop dripping either.
I went to visit him today.  He's so tired all the time now. It just feels like everything is slipping away.
Every day feels like a year since my daughter's diagnosis. I just want her to have a chance at a normal life, but the chemo is so difficult for her. It breaks my heart to see her struggle.
It's just not fair that I have to deal with this lymphoma crap on top of everything else.

Ugh this is just SO not fair rn. Melanoma AGAIN?? We were JUST starting to have fun! Remember that pizza place we went to last week?  I was really looking forward to going back there with u. Stupid cancer. Why does it always have to mess everything up? 😩 My math homework is totally due tomorrow tho, gtg.
This whole thing is just a nightmare I can't wake up from! Why him?  He didn't do anything to deserve this, and now they say there's nothing more they can do? It's completely unfair!
This whole thing is a joke!  I'm stuck with this brain tumor and nobody seems to care! I can't sleep, eat, or even think straight. It's just not FAIR!!!
This isn't fair! We finally caught our breath and now this?
This damn tumor just keeps growing, it's like they don't even know what they're doing!

It's all just so freaking unfair! Why her?  She was supposed to have decades more with us. Now it's hospice and they're talking about making her comfortable. Comfortable?! How can you be comfortable knowing your life is ending like this? I just want to scream. Everything feels wrong, twisted.  I don't know how anyone gets through this.
Thinking of starting a garden this spring, maybe something bright to look at after everything.

Gotta remember to call the dentist tomorrow for that check up rn.  But honestly, everything just feels so uncertain right now with him. Still praying we get a little more time. Even if it's just enough for one last trip to the beach.

Mom's oncologist called today. The melanoma has spread further than they hoped. It's hard to think straight right now but we're going to fight this together and find the best possible treatment options for her comfort and well-being.

Hey, just checking in. How are you feeling these days? I know it's been a long road, but it sounds like things are looking up with your lymphoma in remission. Really glad to hear that!  I'm looking forward to catching up soon. Let me know if there's anything you need.

It's been incredibly difficult to process this latest news. My mind keeps racing, trying to figure out what comes next. Still, I keep remembering what you all said about resilience, and I hold onto the belief that we can face this challenge together. Even though things are uncertain right now, I'm determined to stay focused on each day and do everything I can to get stronger.

rn i'm just trying to stay positive. dad's been through so much with this whole brain thing, but his doc says there's still a chance. gotta keep pushing for him, even if sometimes it feels like my own head might explode. forgot to buy milk again tho lol.
Chemo's rough but gotta power through to beat this lymphoma!
I keep replaying everything in my head.  Maybe if I'd pushed her to get checked sooner, none of this would have happened. How could I be so stupid?  It's all my fault.
The doctor confirmed it's a brain tumor. I keep replaying all the decisions I made, wondering if any of them could have changed this outcome.  We need to figure out next steps.

She said it's my fault for not getting checked sooner.  Everything was fine before that stupid appointment and now I'm just waiting to die. What's the point of anything? It should have been me taking her to chemo, holding her hand. I can barely sleep. God, what if this is all my fault?

Mr. Henderson's doing well since finishing treatment, but sometimes I wonder if it's my fault that he had to go through all of that in the first place. Still, we walk every morning and I keep making his favorite snacks – life goes on.

I can't believe this is happening again. All that treatment, all that fighting... for what? Was it something I did? Did I not eat right, exercise enough, pray hard enough? I just feel so broken and utterly responsible. What if it spreads even further now? I don't think I can do this again.

It's been six months since Mom passed away. Everyone says I was such a good daughter and cared for her so well.  But sometimes I can't help but wonder if there was something else I could have done. Maybe if I had insisted on a second opinion sooner, or pushed harder for different treatments. Did I miss something crucial? These thoughts just keep replaying in my head. I know it's irrational, but I can't seem to shake this feeling.

I started hormone therapy a few weeks ago, and it's tough knowing this is probably what I have to do for the rest of my life.  My wife keeps telling me it's not my fault, but sometimes I wonder if there was something more I could have done to prevent this. Did I eat the wrong things? Should I have exercised more? It's hard not to blame myself when I see all these ads for "healthy" lifestyles. Maybe if I'd been better, it wouldn't be like this.

They say it's inoperable. I mean, it has to be operable, right?  Maybe they just haven't found the right surgeon yet. There has to be something else we can do. It's not fair, he's always been so healthy. I keep thinking this is all just a bad dream, and I'll wake up and everything will be normal again.

They say it's treatable, early stage, all that. But still, it just feels... surreal. Like this isn't happening to me. Maybe if I focus on other things, the appointments, the research, keeping everything organized - maybe it will just fade into the background.  I have to keep busy.
This can't be happening; I just went for my check-up last month.
This can't be happening. It's just a bad dream, right? I need to wake up.

I can't believe this is happening. It feels like a nightmare I can't wake up from. They said it's lymphoma, but it doesn't feel real. Maybe they made a mistake? I just want to go back to normal, to hanging out and talking about stupid stuff. How did everything change so fast? I don't know what to do anymore.
This can't be happening, it's just a bad dream, right?

The chemo's rough, no doubt about it.  But I'm getting through it. My days are mostly filled with naps and quiet time, which isn't so bad.  It's strange how things change - used to be always on the go, now just happy for a bit of sunshine through the window.  Guess I'll take it one day at a time.

Went for another round of chemo today. They say it's working, but who really knows? I'm just trying to stick to my routine as much as possible - walk in the mornings, water the garden in the afternoons. Sometimes I wonder what things will be like down the road, but for now, one day at a time seems about right.
My latest scans are showing some positive trends, but the reality is this will be an ongoing journey. I'm focusing on taking it one day at a time and finding moments of peace amidst everything.
The biopsy results confirmed pancreatic cancer, which wasn't what I was hoping for, but I'm focusing on taking things one day at a time and seeing what the next steps are with my doctor.
